findings.title: Peak Activity Pattern
findings.body:
This is the finding that reveals a critical gap in our understanding. The temporal data for this dataset is **not available** — we don't know when these records occurred, which means we're missing one of the most powerful lenses for understanding patterns in human behavior.

Time tells stories that raw numbers can't. It reveals the rhythm of daily life: when people work, when they travel, when systems get overwhelmed, when mistakes happen. Without timestamps, we can't identify the rush hours that strain capacity, the overnight periods when staffing thins out, the seasonal patterns that repeat year after year, or the slow creep of change over time. We're looking at a snapshot with no context about whether it represents a typical Tuesday afternoon or a chaotic Friday night.

This absence matters because timing shapes everything from resource allocation to prevention strategies. You can't staff appropriately if you don't know when demand peaks. You can't measure improvement if you can't track changes over time. The lack of temporal data doesn't just limit analysis — it fundamentally changes what questions we can answer and what solutions we can propose.

pull_quotes: Without timestamps, we're analyzing human behavior with one of our most powerful lenses completely missing.
Time reveals the rhythm of daily life—when systems strain, when mistakes happen, when patterns repeat year after year.
methodology:
**Methodology**

**Dataset:** `campaign_finance/dark_money_swing_districts_2024.csv` — campaign finance records from swing districts in 2024. Record count not specified.

**Analyses Performed:**

- **Clustering:** Unsupervised learning applied to identify patterns in dark money contributions across swing districts
- **Temporal Analysis:** Time-series examination of contribution flows and spending patterns throughout the 2024 election cycle
- **Additional exploratory analysis** to uncover relationships between funding sources, recipient campaigns, and district characteristics

**Tools:** Python · pandas · scikit-learn · matplotlib/seaborn
